raw_consumer_statement:
The disputed charges - On XX/XX/year> ( spoke with XXXX ), my account was charged XXXX separate transactions of {$99.00} each and one at {$9.00}, posted within minutes of XXXX another over several hours, totaling approximately {$15000.00}. I did not make, authorize, or benefit from any of these transactions. The pattern - identical amounts, identical merchant, rapid succession- is consistent with automated fraud. These charges caused the account to exceed its credit limit by approximately {$5000.00}. 

My report - I notified Citi by telephone on XX/XX/year>, one day after the charges occurred and while they were still pending. Citi had not independently detected or acted on the charges before my call. At my instruction, the card was cancelled and reissued. However, I have received no written acknowledgment from Citi that a billing dispute was opened pertaining to these charges. Citi has not provided any dispute ID or reference number, and the billing dispute is not listed in the Billing Disputes section of the cardholder website or within the cardholder mobile application. I spoke with XXXX from Citi 's Executive Response Board on Sunday, XX/XX/XXXX. She said there is no dispute number and she is unable to issue one. 

A related concern - In reviewing my credit reports I found several account-review ( soft ) inquiries placed by Citi in the days immediately preceding the fraud. I have not applied for any Citi product in recent months and was ineligible for a credit-line increase, having received one XXXX months ago. I requested a written explanation of the purpose of these inquiries from Citi as they were unable to explain them.
